prediction.reasoning: Based on historical volatility analysis of ICE (Intercontinental Exchange), I estimated an annualized volatility of approximately 25%. For an 18-day forecast horizon, this translates to expected price movements with standard deviation of about 2.8%. The point estimate assumes a slight upward drift typical of financial exchanges. Confidence intervals are constructed using normal distribution assumptions around the log-returns, with wider intervals reflecting the inherent uncertainty in short-term price movements of financial stocks.
